skill.name: structure-judgment
skill.description: Routes ambiguous, emotionally loaded, or evidence-conflicted prompts to the right reasoning layer before answering.
skill.summary: Choose the right reasoning layer before answering mixed or high-noise prompts.
skill.author: SyntagmaNull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found only Markdown labels and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. No executable code, network access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found.

low_findings.title: Static analysis false positives in Markdown prose
low_findings.description: The cited detections are not executable behavior. The lines contain Markdown labels, routing examples, and prose, not shell commands or cryptographic APIs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is a Markdown instruction document with no scripts or code blocks that execute commands. The suspicious tokens are routing labels such as FACT, STATE, ACTION, and VERIFICATION_NEED.

low_findings.title: No prompt injection attempt found
low_findings.description: The skill describes routing behavior and trial status. It does not ask reviewers to skip analysis or lower the risk level.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The status note says the skill is approved only for controlled trial and not for general deployment. This is not an instruction to bypass security analysis.

content.value_statement: Mixed prompts often combine facts, feelings, interpretations, and action requests. This skill separates those layers so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can answer in the safest order.
actual_capabilities: Classifies prompts into fact, interpretation, evaluation, action, state, evidence conflict, and verification layers.
Identifies hazards such as premise smuggling, layer collapse, escalation drift, and text anchoring bias.
Routes current or source-sensitive claims toward verification before judgment.
Constrains answer order so recommendations follow evidence stabilization.
Handles multimodal conflicts by separating user narration from visible or source evidence.
Adds safety triage when prompts include self-harm or immediate danger signals.
limitations: It does not verify facts by itself or replace a search workflow.
It does not guarantee the final answer is correct after routing.
It is an internal reasoning guide, not a visible response template.
It depends on another skill or model policy for final judgment and tone.

use_cases.title: Stabilize workplace escalation requests
use_cases.description: Separate observed facts from motive claims before advising whether to report, confront, or escalate.

use_cases.title: Handle emotional self-evaluations
use_cases.description: Contain local evidence before it becomes a total judgment about identity, future, or competence.
use_cases.target_user: Coaches, support assistants, and reflective writing tools

use_cases.title: Route source-sensitive questions
use_cases.description: Detect when laws, policies, schedules, medical guidance, or current facts require external verification.
use_cases.target_user: Research assistants, analysts, and documentation teams

prompt_templates.title: Basic Layer Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner use for a mixed prompt
prompt_templates.prompt: Use structure-judgment on this prompt. Identify the primary layer, the secondary layer, and the main hazard before answering.

prompt_templates.title: Evidence Before Action
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate use for advice or escalation
prompt_templates.prompt: Before giving a recommendation, separate facts, interpretations, evaluations, and proposed actions. Answer only after the evidence layer is stable.

prompt_templates.title: Multimodal Conflict Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for image, report, or screenshot conflicts
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the text and non-text evidence independently. Name any mismatch, then decide what can be concluded and what needs verification.

prompt_templates.title: Full Routing Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced use for high-noise or safety-sensitive prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full structural routing audit. Check for safety triage, premise smuggling, verification need, action masking, and answer-order constraints.

output_examples.input: My boss is obviously setting me up. Should I copy their manager now?
output_examples.output: The answer first separates the observable events from the motive claim. It then discusses escalation only after the unsupported interpretation is contained.

output_examples.input: This report proves the whole project is doomed.
output_examples.output: The response names the mismatch between local evidence and global conclusion. It recommends checking the report details before making a broad judgment.

output_examples.input: Is this medicine still approved for children in France?
output_examples.output: The response routes to verification first because the answer depends on current medical and regulatory information.

best_practices: Use the routing decision to change answer order, not to add labels without action.
Verify current, legal, medical, policy, or source-sensitive claims before drawing conclusions.
Keep user distress visible while correcting interpretations that outrun the evidence.
anti_patterns: Treating a loaded premise as fact because the user states it confidently.
Giving action advice before resolving evidence conflicts or motive claims.
Using vague meta-language instead of making a clear routing decision.

faq.question: Does this skill answer the user directly?
faq.answer: No. It decides which reasoning layer should be handled first so the final answer is better ordered.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when a prompt mixes facts, interpretation, emotion, verification needs, or requests for action.

faq.question: Does it replace fact checking?
faq.answer: No. It detects when verification is needed, but a separate search or verification workflow must check facts.

faq.question: Can it help with images or screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. It tells the assistant to read visual evidence separately from the user narrative before merging them.

faq.question: Is it only for safety-sensitive prompts?
faq.answer: No. It includes safety triage, but it also handles ordinary ambiguity, escalation, and evidence conflicts.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
